question: Find the Missouri state agency that ranked third in average spending from FY2007 to FY2010. Find the city the Southwest district of the agency is based and a highway that was born in the city. Between that highway's two terminus cities, which one had higher binge drinking rates in the year (within 2011-2016) that has the lowest teen birth rate for females ages 15-19 across all races in the United States and the year (within 2018-2019) that has the lowest total ridership for Bloomington Transit bus routes? For that city, identify the department with the second-highest average overtime costs from the year (within 2011-2016) that has the lowest age-adjusted death rate for all races and both sexes in the United States to the year (within 2017-2022) that has the highest average annual PM2.5 mean in New York City, and state its founding year. Please return the year only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Filtering to Race = "All Races" and Sex = "Both Sexes" for 2011-2016, the minimum Age-adjusted Death Rate is 724.6 in 2014.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2011-2016 has the lowest age-adjusted death rate for all races and both sexes in the United States? (Filter Race == "All Races" and Sex == "Both Sexes"; select the minimum Age-adjusted Death Rate.)

2.fact: Filtering to Race or Hispanic Origin = "All Races" and Age Group = "15-19 Years" for 2011-2016, the minimum Birth Rate is 20.3 in 2016.
2.subquestion: Which year from 2011-2016 has the lowest teen birth rate for females ages 15-19 across all races in the United States? (Filter Race or Hispanic Origin == "All Races" and Age Group == "15-19 Years"; select the minimum Birth Rate.)

3.fact: Summing riders by year for 2018-2019, the minimum total is 3106507 in 2018.
3.subquestion: Which year from 2018-2019 has the lowest total ridership for Bloomington Transit bus routes? (Use ts to derive year; sum riders by year and select the minimum.)

4.fact: Filtering to Name = "Fine particles (PM 2.5)", Measure = "Mean", and Time Period containing "Annual Average" for 2017-2022, the highest average Data Value is 7.74113475177305 in 2017.
4.subquestion: Which year from 2017-2022 in New York City has the highest average annual PM2.5 mean? (Filter Name == "Fine particles (PM 2.5)", Measure == "Mean", Time Period contains "Annual Average"; average Data Value by year and select the maximum.)

5.fact: In FY2007 Missouri State Expenditures, the top 5 agencies by total expenditure were: SOCIAL SERVICES ($5,528,171,459.84), ELEMENTARY AND SECONDARY EDUCATION ($4,893,252,163.41), TRANSPORTATION ($1,922,237,354.88), HIGHER EDUCATION AND WORKFORCE DEV ($1,062,578,010.95), and MENTAL HEALTH ($759,892,459.14).
5.subquestion: Which Missouri state agency had the 3rd highest average expenditure from FY2007-FY2010?

6.fact: In FY2008 Missouri State Expenditures, the top 5 agencies by total expenditure were: SOCIAL SERVICES ($5,988,522,037.62), ELEMENTARY AND SECONDARY EDUCATION ($5,048,553,721.08), TRANSPORTATION ($1,786,534,604.37), HIGHER EDUCATION AND WORKFORCE DEV ($1,220,312,581.65), and MENTAL HEALTH ($813,799,778.73).
6.subquestion: Which Missouri state agency had the 3rd highest average expenditure from FY2007-FY2010?

7.fact: In FY2009 Missouri State Expenditures, the top 5 agencies by total expenditure were: SOCIAL SERVICES ($6,707,806,742.86), ELEMENTARY AND SECONDARY EDUCATION ($5,170,194,688.51), TRANSPORTATION ($2,000,940,522.19), HIGHER EDUCATION AND WORKFORCE DEV ($1,291,298,943.33), and MENTAL HEALTH ($877,438,429.19).
7.subquestion: Which Missouri state agency had the 3rd highest average expenditure from FY2007-FY2010?

8.fact: In FY2010 Missouri State Expenditures, the top 5 agencies by total expenditure were: SOCIAL SERVICES ($7,141,666,880.32), ELEMENTARY AND SECONDARY EDUCATION ($5,351,561,573.54), TRANSPORTATION ($2,144,139,919.08), HIGHER EDUCATION AND WORKFORCE DEV ($1,320,888,790.61), and MENTAL HEALTH ($921,056,914.52).
8.subquestion: Which Missouri state agency had the 3rd highest average expenditure from FY2007-FY2010?

9.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Missouri Department of Transportation, 'MoDOT operates seven districts throughout the state... Southwest, based in Springfield.'
9.subquestion: In which city is the Southwest district of the agency (from hop 3: TRANSPORTATION/MoDOT) based?

10.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Springfield, Missouri, 'Springfield's nicknames include "The Birthplace of Route 66"...it was in Springfield that officials first proposed the name of the new Chicago-to-Los Angeles highway.'
10.subquestion: What are the terminus cities of the highway that was born in the city (from hop 4: Springfield)?

11.fact: In the 500-Cities <node_2 answer> health data release, the binge drinking prevalence (city-level, crude) was: Chicago 20.1% and Los Angeles 15.9%. Chicago had higher binge drinking prevalence.
11.subquestion: Which terminus city (from hop 5: Chicago or Los Angeles) had higher average binge drinking prevalence across the <node_2 answer> and <node_3 answer> 500-Cities health releases?

12.fact: In the 500-Cities <node_3 answer> health data release, the binge drinking prevalence (city-level, crude) was: Chicago 21.5% and Los Angeles 16.7%. Chicago had higher binge drinking prevalence.
12.subquestion: Which terminus city (from hop 5: Chicago or Los Angeles) had higher average binge drinking prevalence across the <node_2 answer> and <node_3 answer> 500-Cities health releases?

13.fact: In the 2014 Chicago Employee Overtime data, the top departments by total overtime were: Police ($101,460,132.24), Fire ($63,941,565.36), Water Management ($23,507,061.70).
13.subquestion: Which city department had the 2nd highest total overtime from <node_1 answer> to <node_4 answer> in the city (from hop 6: Chicago)?

14.subquestion: Which city department had the 2nd highest total overtime from <node_1 answer> to <node_4 answer> in the city (from hop 6: Chicago)?

15.fact: In the 2016 Chicago Employee Overtime data, the top departments by total overtime were: Police ($143,032,724.45), Fire ($50,604,010.67), Water Management ($21,335,912.52).
15.subquestion: Which city department had the 2nd highest total overtime from <node_1 answer> to <node_4 answer> in the city (from hop 6: Chicago)?

16.fact: In the 2017 Chicago Employee Overtime data, Fire was consistently 2nd across all 4 years (2014-2017).
16.subquestion: Which city department had the 2nd highest total overtime from <node_1 answer> to <node_4 answer> in the city (from hop 6: Chicago)?

17.subquestion: In what year was the department (from hop 7: Fire) founded?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Year with lowest age-adjusted death rate (2011-2016) -> 2014
Node 2: Year with lowest teen birth rate (2011-2016) -> 2016
Node 3: Year with lowest Bloomington Transit ridership (2018-2019) -> 2018
Node 4: Year with highest NYC PM2.5 mean (2017-2022) -> 2017
